question: Which timeseries identifiers correspond to the supply air temperature measurement, supply air flow measurement, and flow setpoint of the AHU supplying air to a space?
original_sparql: PREFIX brick: <https://brickschema.org/schema/Brick#>PREFIX ref: <https://brickschema.org/schema/Brick/ref#>SELECT ?ahu ?tempMeas ?tempTimeseriesId ?flowMeas ?flowTimeseriesId ?flowSetpoint ?flowSpTimeseriesId WHERE { ?ahu a brick:AHU . OPTIONAL { ?ahu brick:hasPoint ?tempMeas . ?tempMeas a brick:Supply_Air_Temperature_Measurement . ?tempMeas ref:hasTimeseriesId ?tempTimeseriesId . } OPTIONAL { ?ahu brick:hasPoint ?flowMeas . ?flowMeas a brick:Supply_Air_Flow_Measurement . ?flowMeas ref:hasTimeseriesId ?flowTimeseriesId . } OPTIONAL { ?ahu brick:hasPoint ?flowSetpoint . ?flowSetpoint a brick:Supply_Air_Flow_Setpoint . ?flowSetpoint ref:hasTimeseriesId ?flowSpTimeseriesId . } }

question: For each labeled HVAC zone, list its name and all associated timeseries identifiers for temperature measurements, various setpoint targets (min, max, heating, cooling, occupied and unoccupied), occupancy sensing and command, as well as the VAV unit’s damper position, discharge temperature, and reheat control signals.
original_sparql: PREFIX brick: <https://brickschema.org/schema/Brick#>PREFIX rdf: <http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#>PREFIX rdfs: <http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#>SELECT ?zoneEntity ?zoneTempSensorClass ?heatingSetpointClass ?coolingSetpointClass ?occupiedSetpointClass ?commandClass ?damperPart WHERE {?zoneEntity rdf:type brick:Variable_Air_Volume_Box_With_Reheat .BIND(brick:Zone_Air_Temperature_Sensor AS ?zoneTempSensorClass)BIND(brick:Zone_Air_Heating_Temperature_Setpoint AS ?heatingSetpointClass)BIND(brick:Zone_Air_Cooling_Temperature_Setpoint AS ?coolingSetpointClass)BIND(brick:Occupied_Zone_Air_Temperature_Setpoint AS ?occupiedSetpointClass)BIND(brick:Command AS ?commandClass)OPTIONAL {?zoneEntity brick:hasPart ?damperPart .?damperPart rdf:type brick:Damper .}}

question: What devices are fitted with zone temperature sensors and what are those sensors?

FILTER (?zoneType = brick:Zone) . ?sensorType brick:hasAssociatedTag tag:Zone ; brick:hasAssociatedTag tag:Temperature ; brick:hasAssociatedTag tag:Sensor ; rdfs:label ?sensorLabel . }

question: What are the Supply Air Temperature Sensors and the equipment they belong to?
original_sparql: PREFIX brick: <https://brickschema.org/schema/Brick#> PREFIX rdfs: <http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#> SELECT ?sensor ?label ?equipmentName WHERE { ?sensor a brick:Supply_Air_Temperature_Sensor . OPTIONAL { ?sensor rdfs:label ?label . } BIND(STRAFTER(STR(?sensor), "#") AS ?localName) BIND(REPLACE(?localName, "bldg11\\.[^.]+\\.([^.]+)\\..*", "$1") AS ?equipmentName) FILTER(BOUND(?equipmentName) && ?equipmentName != ?localName) }

question: Is there any simultaneous heating and cooling fault in any of my AHUs?
original_sparql:
PREFIX tag: <https://brickschema.org/schema/BrickTag#>
PREFIX rdf: <http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#>

SELECT DISTINCT ?entity
WHERE {
    ?entity rdf:type tag:AHU .
    ?entity rdf:type tag:Heating .
    ?entity rdf:type tag:Cool .
    ?entity rdf:type tag:Fault .
}

question: What are the labels of each underfloor terminal, the label of the zone whose domain space it is connected to, and the label of the air handling unit supplying it?

SELECT ?underfloorTerminalLabel ?zoneLabel ?ahuLabel
WHERE {
    brick:Underfloor_Air_Plenum rdfs:label ?underfloorTerminalLabel .

question: What timeseries identifiers are linked to electric power sensors listed in the baseline table through an external reference?
original_sparql:
PREFIX ref: <https://brickschema.org/schema/Brick/ref#>
PREFIX brick: <https://brickschema.org/schema/Brick#>
PREFIX rdfs: <http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#>
PREFIX owl: <http://www.w3.org/2002/07/owl#>

SELECT ?timeseriesIdentifier ?electricPowerSensor ?externalReference
WHERE {
    ref:TimeseriesReference rdfs:label ?timeseriesIdentifier .
    brick:Electric_Power_Sensor a owl:Class .
    ref:ExternalReference a owl:Class .
    BIND(brick:Electric_Power_Sensor AS ?electricPowerSensor)
    BIND(ref:ExternalReference AS ?externalReference)
}

question: What are the non-writable ALC and MPC endpoints and their related property names for all water-to-water heat pumps?
original_sparql:
PREFIX brick: <https://brickschema.org/schema/Brick#>
PREFIX s223: <http://data.ashrae.org/standard223#>
PREFIX xsd: <http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#>

SELECT DISTINCT ?endpoint ?propertyName
WHERE {
  ?hp a brick:Water_to_Water_Heat_Pump .
  ?hp brick:hasPoint ?point .
  ?controller brick:controls ?point .
  ?controller a ?controllerType .
  FILTER (?controllerType = brick:ALC || ?controllerType = brick:MPC) .
  ?point s223:hasEndpoint ?endpoint .
  OPTIONAL { ?endpoint s223:isWritable ?isWritable . }
  FILTER (!BOUND(?isWritable) || ?isWritable = "false"^^xsd:boolean) .
  OPTIONAL { ?endpoint s223:propertyName ?propertyName . }
}

PREFIX s223: <http://data.ashrae.org/standard223#>

OPTIONAL { ?ahu rdfs:label ?ahuLabel . } ?ahu brick:feeds+ ?device . ?device a brick:Air_Distribution_Device . OPTIONAL { ?device rdfs:label ?deviceLabel . } { ?device brick:hasLocation ?plenum . ?plenum a brick:Underfloor_Air_Plenum . } UNION { ?plenum brick:hasPart ?device . ?plenum a brick:Underfloor_Air_Plenum . } OPTIONAL { ?plenum rdfs:label ?plenumLabel . } ?device brick:serves+ ?zone . ?zone a brick:Zone . question: What non-writable external endpoints and MPC database names are associated with measurements taken by components inside water-to-water heat pumps?
original_sparql: PREFIX brick: <https://brickschema.org/schema/Brick#>PREFIX s223: <http://data.ashrae.org/standard223#>PREFIX sosa: <http://www.w3.org/ns/sosa#>PREFIX rdfs: <http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#>SELECT DISTINCT ?externalEndpoint ?databaseName WHERE {    ?hp a brick:Water_to_Water_Heat_Pump .    ?hp brick:hasPart ?component .    ?point a ?pointType .    ?pointType rdfs:subClassOf* brick:Point .    ?point brick:isPointOf ?component .    {        ?point a sosa:Sensor .        BIND(?point AS ?measurementSource)    } UNION {        ?sensor sosa:observes ?point .        BIND(?sensor AS ?measurementSource)    } UNION {        ?observation sosa:observes ?point .        BIND(?observation AS ?measurementSource)    }    OPTIONAL {        ?measurementSource s223:hasEndpoint ?endpoint .        ?endpoint s223:hasExternalEndpoint ?externalEndpoint .        FILTER NOT EXISTS { ?endpoint s223:isWritable true }    }    OPTIONAL {        ?measurementSource s223:hasDatabase ?database .        ?database s223:hasDatabaseName ?databaseName .    }}

question: For each HVAC zone in the building, what are the timeseries IDs for the available zone temperature sensor' points, min and max temperature setpoints, temperature setpoints (single, heating/cooling or occupied/unoccupied), occupancy sensors and commands, and any related VAV damper, discharge temperature, and reheat command points?
original_sparql: PREFIX brick: <https://brickschema.org/schema/Brick#> PREFIX ref: <https://brickschema.org/schema/Brick/ref#> SELECT ?zone ?ts_id ?sp_min_id ?sp_max_id ?sp_single_id ?sp_heating_id ?sp_cooling_id ?sp_occupied_id ?sp_unoccupied_id ?os_id ?cmd_id ?vav_id ?discharge_id ?reheat_id WHERE { ?zone a brick:HVAC_Zone . OPTIONAL { ?zone brick:hasPoint ?ts . ?ts a brick:Temperature_Sensor ; brick:hasPointId ?ts_id . } OPTIONAL { ?zone brick:hasPoint ?sp_min . ?sp_min a brick:Temperature_Setpoint_Min ; brick:hasPointId ?sp_min_id . } OPTIONAL { ?zone brick:hasPoint ?sp_max . ?sp_max a brick:Temperature_Setpoint_Max ; brick:hasPointId ?sp_max_id . } OPTIONAL { ?zone brick:hasPoint ?sp_single . ?sp_single a brick:Temperature_Setpoint_Single ; brick:hasPointId ?sp_single_id . } OPTIONAL { ?zone brick:hasPoint ?sp_heating . ?sp_heating a brick:Temperature_Setpoint_Heating ; brick:hasPointId ?sp_heating_id . } OPTIONAL { ?zone brick:hasPoint ?sp_cooling . ?sp_cooling a brick:Temperature_Setpoint_Cooling ; brick:hasPointId ?sp_cooling_id . } OPTIONAL { ?zone brick:hasPoint ?sp_occupied . ?sp_occupied a brick:Temperature_Setpoint_Occupied ; brick:hasPointId ?sp_occupied_id . } OPTIONAL { ?zone brick:hasPoint ?sp_unoccupied . ?sp_unoccupied a brick:Temperature_Setpoint_Unoccupied ; brick:hasPointId ?sp_unoccupied_id . } OPTIONAL { ?zone brick:hasPoint ?os . ?os a brick:Occupancy_Sensor ; brick:hasPointId ?os_id . } OPTIONAL { ?zone brick:hasPoint ?cmd . ?cmd a brick:Command ; brick:hasPointId ?cmd_id . } OPTIONAL { ?zone brick:hasPoint ?vav . ?vav a brick:VAV_Damper ; brick:hasPointId ?vav_id . } OPTIONAL { ?zone brick:hasPoint ?discharge . ?discharge a brick:Discharge_Temperature_Sensor ; brick:hasPointId ?discharge_id . } OPTIONAL { ?zone brick:hasPoint ?reheat . ?reheat a brick:Reheat_Command ; brick:hasPointId ?reheat_id . } }

question: How do I access the supply air measurement data for an AHU?
original_sparql:
PREFIX brick: <https://brickschema.org/schema/Brick#> 
PREFIX rdf: <http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#> 
PREFIX rdfs: <http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#> 
SELECT ?supply_air_measurement 
WHERE { 
    ?ahu a brick:AHU . 
    ?supply_air_measurement a brick:Supply_Air . 
    ?ahu ?p ?supply_air_measurement . 
    ?p rdfs:label ?label . 
    FILTER ( REGEX ( ?label, "supply air", "i" ) ) . 
}

question: What timeseries IDs are used for the temperature setpoints of each building zone?
original_sparql:
PREFIX brick: <https://brickschema.org/schema/Brick#>
PREFIX rdfs: <http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#>

# Traverse from Building through Storey to Zone and then retrieve its temperature setpoint timeseries
SELECT ?building ?storey ?zone ?timeseriesID
WHERE {
  ?building a brick:Building .
  ?building brick:hasPart ?storey .
  ?storey a brick:Storey .
  ?storey brick:hasPart ?zone .
  ?zone a brick:Zone .
  ?zone brick:hasPart ?setpoint .
  ?setpoint a brick:Temperature_Setpoint .
  BIND(STR(?setpoint) AS ?timeseriesID)
}

}

question: How much floor space do the buildings occupy?

PREFIX brick: <https://brickschema.org/schema/Brick#>
PREFIX ns1: <http://qudt.org/schema/qudt/>
PREFIX rdfs: <http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#>

SELECT ?zone ?setpoint ?setpointLabel ?quantityKind ?device ?deviceLabel
WHERE {
  { ?setpoint a brick:Target_Zone_Air_Temperature_Setpoint } UNION
  { ?setpoint a brick:Occupied_Target_Zone_Air_Temperature_Setpoint } UNION
  { ?setpoint a brick:Standby_Target_Zone_Air_Temperature_Setpoint } UNION
  { ?setpoint a brick:Effective_Target_Zone_Air_Temperature_Setpoint } UNION
  { ?setpoint a brick:Unoccupied_Target_Zone_Air_Temperature_Setpoint } .
  ?setpoint brick:hasSubstance ?zone .
  OPTIONAL { ?setpoint rdfs:label ?setpointLabel . }
  ?setpoint ns1:hasQuantityKind ?quantityKind .
  OPTIONAL {
    ?setpoint brick:hasAssociatedTag ?tag .
    ?device brick:isAssociatedWith ?tag .
    OPTIONAL { ?device rdfs:label ?deviceLabel . }
    FILTER(CONTAINS(STR(?tag), "Supply"))
  }
}
ORDER BY ?zone

question: What equipment has setpoints for zone air temperature and what are they?
original_sparql:
PREFIX brick: <https://brickschema.org/schema/Brick#>
PREFIX tag: <https://brickschema.org/schema/BrickTag#>
PREFIX owl: <http://www.w3.org/2002/07/owl#>
PREFIX rdfs: <http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#>

# Query 4: Retrieve equipment (using tag association) along with their associated zone air temperature setpoints
# Also retrieve the QUDT quantity reference from the setpoint via brick:hasQuantity if available
SELECT DISTINCT ?equipment ?equipmentLabel ?setpoint ?setpointLabel ?quantity
WHERE {
  # Equipment from tag
  tag:Equipment brick:isAssociatedWith ?equipment .
  ?equipment a brick:Equipment ;
             rdfs:label ?equipmentLabel .

  # Temperature setpoints from tag with filtering on setpoint label
  tag:Temperature brick:isAssociatedWith ?setpoint .
  ?setpoint a owl:Class ;
            rdfs:label ?setpointLabel .
  FILTER(regex(?setpointLabel, "Zone Air Temperature Setpoint", "i"))

  OPTIONAL { ?setpoint brick:hasQuantity ?quantity }
}
ORDER BY ?equipmentLabel ?setpointLabel

PREFIX brick: <https://brickschema.org/schema/Brick#>
PREFIX owl: <http://www.w3.org/2002/07/owl#>
PREFIX rdfs: <http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#>
PREFIX ns1: <http://www.kgqa.com/>

SELECT ?zone (GROUP_CONCAT(?tempLabel; separator=", ") AS ?TemperatureStreams)
WHERE {
  ?zone a brick:Zone .
  ?tempStream a owl:Class ;
              rdfs:label ?tempLabel ;
              ns1:count ?count .
  FILTER(CONTAINS(?tempLabel, "Temperature"))
}
GROUP BY ?zone
ORDER BY ?zone

question: What are the non-writable ALC and MPC endpoints and their related property names for all water-to-water heat pumps?
original_sparql:
PREFIX s223: <http://data.ashrae.org/standard223#>
PREFIX ref: <https://brickschema.org/schema/Brick/>
PREFIX bob: <http://data.ashrae.org/standard223/si-builder#>
PREFIX rdfs: <http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#>

SELECT DISTINCT ?equipment ?property ?propertyName ?endpointSource ?endpointMPCName
WHERE {
    ?equipment a s223:Equipment .

    # Identify "water-to-water" heat pumps by having at least two water connection points
    # Note: Based on the provided graph, this condition might not yield any results
    # as no equipment is explicitly shown with two bob:WaterConnectionPoint instances.
    ?equipment s223:hasConnectionPoint ?cp1 .
    ?cp1 a bob:WaterConnectionPoint .
    ?equipment s223:hasConnectionPoint ?cp2 .
    ?cp2 a bob:WaterConnectionPoint .
    FILTER (?cp1 != ?cp2)

    # Find properties associated with this equipment
    ?equipment s223:hasProperty ?property .

    # Filter for non-writable properties (endpoints)
    ?property ref:writable "False" .

    # Filter for ALC or MPC endpoints
    # Note: Based on the provided graph, no property satisfies both
    # ref:writable "False" AND (ref:source "ALC SOAP API" OR ref:mpc_db_name exists).
    # Therefore, this part of the query will likely result in an empty set.
    {
        ?property ref:source "ALC SOAP API" .
        BIND ("ALC" AS ?endpointSource)
    }
    UNION
    {
        ?property ref:mpc_db_name ?mpcName .
        BIND (?mpcName AS ?endpointMPCName)
    }

    # Get the related property name (rdfs:label or ref:mpc_db_name)
    OPTIONAL { ?property rdfs:label ?label . }
    BIND(COALESCE(?label, ?mpcName, "No Name Provided") AS ?propertyName)
}

question: Which time series should I pull to see how each zone and its VAV are performing in terms of temperature control and occupancy?
original_sparql:
PREFIX brick: <https://brickschema.org/schema/Brick#>
PREFIX ref: <https://brickschema.org/schema/Brick/ref#>

SELECT DISTINCT ?zone ?vav ?point ?pointType ?timeseriesId ?storedAt ?onTable
WHERE {
    ?zone a brick:HVAC_Zone .
    ?vav a brick:VAV .
    ?vav brick:feeds ?zone .

    { 
        ?zone brick:hasPoint ?point .
        ?point a ?pointType .
        FILTER (?pointType IN (
            brick:Zone_Air_Temperature_Sensor,
            brick:Zone_Air_Temperature_Setpoint,
            brick:Occupancy_Sensor,
            brick:Min_Air_Temperature_Setpoint,
            brick:Max_Air_Temperature_Setpoint,
            brick:Zone_Air_Heating_Temperature_Setpoint,
            brick:Zone_Air_Cooling_Temperature_Setpoint
        ))
    }
    UNION
    { 
        ?vav brick:hasPoint ?point .
        ?point a ?pointType .
        FILTER (?pointType IN (
            brick:Damper_Position_Setpoint,
            brick:Damper_Position_Sensor,
            brick:Damper_Command,
            brick:Valve_Command,
            brick:Valve_Position_Sensor,
            brick:Discharge_Air_Flow_Sensor,
            brick:Discharge_Air_Temperature_Sensor
        ))
    }

    ?point ref:hasExternalReference ?extRef .
    OPTIONAL { ?extRef ref:hasTimeseriesId ?timeseriesId } .
    OPTIONAL { ?extRef ref:storedAt ?storedAt } .
    OPTIONAL { ?extRef ref:onTable ?onTable } .
}

question: For each HVAC unit, what temperature sensor is installed and what setpoint does it have for controlling room air temperature?
original_sparql:
PREFIX brick: <https://brickschema.org/schema/Brick#>
PREFIX ns2: <http://buildsys.org/ontologies/bldg11#>
PREFIX rdfs: <http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#>

SELECT DISTINCT ?hvac_unit ?temp_sensor ?temp_setpoint
WHERE {
  VALUES ?hvac_unit {
    ns2:AHU01
    ns2:AHU07
    ns2:VAVRM2519
    ns2:VAVRM3311
    ns2:VAVRM3406
    ns2:VAVRM3503
    ns2:VAVRM6117-1
  }

  {
    # Case 1: VAV units - find Zone_Air_Temperature_Sensor and Zone_Air_Temperature_Setpoint
    # VAVs directly control room air temperature in a specific zone.
    ?hvac_unit a brick:VAV .
    ?hvac_unit brick:hasPoint ?temp_sensor .
    ?temp_sensor a brick:Zone_Air_Temperature_Sensor .

    ?hvac_unit brick:hasPoint ?temp_setpoint .
    ?temp_setpoint a brick:Zone_Air_Temperature_Setpoint .
  }
  UNION
  {
    # Case 2: AHU units - find Supply_Air_Temperature_Setpoint and associated Zone_Air_Temperature_Sensors
    # AHUs influence room air temperature by controlling supply air, and serve multiple zones.
    ?hvac_unit a brick:AHU .

    # Find the AHU's primary temperature setpoint for influencing room temperature
    ?hvac_unit brick:hasPoint ?temp_setpoint .
    ?temp_setpoint a brick:Supply_Air_Temperature_Setpoint .

    # Find Zone_Air_Temperature_Sensors in zones served by this AHU.
    # These are the 'room air temperature' sensors that the AHU's control ultimately affects.
    OPTIONAL {
      ?hvac_unit brick:feeds ?zone .
      ?zone brick:hasPoint ?temp_sensor .
      ?temp_sensor a brick:Zone_Air_Temperature_Sensor .
    }
  }
}

question: List the rooms and their hvac equipment that contains a temperature sensor
original_sparql:
PREFIX brick: <https://brickschema.org/schema/Brick#>
PREFIX rdfs: <http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#>
PREFIX ns2: <http://buildsys.org/ontologies/bldg11#>

SELECT DISTINCT ?room ?hvacEquipment
WHERE {
  ?hvacEquipment brick:hasPoint ?point .

  # Identify points related to temperature using label or URI as a heuristic,
  # since explicit brick:Temperature_Sensor types are not present in the graph.
  OPTIONAL { ?point rdfs:label ?pointLabel . }
  FILTER (
    (BOUND(?pointLabel) && CONTAINS(LCASE(STR(?pointLabel)), "temp")) ||
    CONTAINS(LCASE(STR(?point)), "temp")
  ) .

  # Link HVAC equipment to rooms using the brick:feeds+ property.
  # This property is used in the provided graph to connect equipment to spaces/other equipment.
  ?hvacEquipment brick:feeds+ ?room .

  # Filter for entities that are actual rooms based on their specific URI pattern.
  # This regex ensures that only URIs like ns2:RMXXXX are matched, excluding VAV equipment.
  FILTER (REGEX(STR(?room), "^ns2:RM\\d+$")) .
}

question: For each Water-to-Water Heat Pump, what are the related non-writable property labels and ALC and MPC endpoint references?
original_sparql:
PREFIX ref: <https://brickschema.org/schema/Brick/>
PREFIX s223: <http://data.ashrae.org/standard223#>
PREFIX rdfs: <http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#>

# This query returns one row per Water-to-Water Heat Pump (using the Brick class ref:WaterToWaterHeatPump). It collects the labels of its non–writable property points (i.e. those attached via ref:hasPoint that are not writable, here filtered by excluding points of type ref:Command), and it aggregates the pump’s associated external endpoint references. An endpoint whose ref:source equals "ALC SOAP API" is treated as an ALC endpoint; an external reference of type s223:ExternalReference that does not have that source is treated as an MPC endpoint.

SELECT ?pump
       (GROUP_CONCAT(DISTINCT ?nonWritableLabel; separator=", ") AS ?nonWritableProperties)
       (GROUP_CONCAT(DISTINCT ?alcEndpoint; separator=", ") AS ?alcEndpoints)
       (GROUP_CONCAT(DISTINCT ?mpcEndpoint; separator=", ") AS ?mpcEndpoints)
WHERE {
  # Identify pumps explicitly as Water-to-Water Heat Pumps using the Brick class
  ?pump a ref:WaterToWaterHeatPump .

  # Retrieve property points (labels) that are considered non-writable (i.e. exclude command points)
  OPTIONAL {
    ?pump ref:hasPoint ?pt .
    ?pt rdfs:label ?nonWritableLabel .
    FILTER NOT EXISTS { ?pt a ref:Command } .
  }

  # ALC endpoint references: via an external reference with ref:source "ALC SOAP API"
  OPTIONAL {
    ?pump ref:hasExternalReference ?alcEndpoint .
    ?alcEndpoint ref:source "ALC SOAP API" .
  }

  # MPC endpoint references: external references (typed as s223:ExternalReference) that do not carry the ALC source
  OPTIONAL {
    ?pump ref:hasExternalReference ?mpcEndpoint .
    ?mpcEndpoint a s223:ExternalReference .
    FILTER NOT EXISTS { ?mpcEndpoint ref:source "ALC SOAP API" } .
  }
}
GROUP BY ?pump

question: How can I retrieve the timeseries IDs for an AHU’s supply air temperature, airflow rate readings, and its airflow setpoint?
original_sparql:
PREFIX bldg1: <urn:bldg/>
PREFIX brick: <https://brickschema.org/schema/Brick#>
PREFIX ref: <https://brickschema.org/schema/Brick/ref#>

# This query uses Brick’s semantics to fetch three kinds of timeseries IDs associated with an AHU. 
# It assumes that the AHU (bldg1:hvac_ahu) is linked via brick:hasPoint to its measurement points and that each point 
# is explicitly typed using Brick classes. In particular, we assume the following types exist in the dataset:
#   • brick:Supply_Air_Temperature_Sensor for the AHU’s supply air temperature sensor,
#   • brick:Supply_Air_Flow_Sensor for the AHU’s airflow rate sensor, and
#   • brick:Supply_Air_Flow_Setpoint for the AHU’s airflow setpoint control.
# Each of these points carries an external reference (using ref:hasExternalReference) that in turn provides the 
# timeseries ID via ref:hasTimeseriesId. This structure avoids brittle IRI string matching and leverages the 
# explicit typing in Brick Schema and ASHRAE 223P.

SELECT ?measurement ?timeseriesId
WHERE {
  {
    # Retrieve the AHU supply air temperature timeseries ID
    bldg1:hvac_ahu brick:hasPoint ?tempPoint .
    ?tempPoint a brick:Supply_Air_Temperature_Sensor .
    ?tempPoint ref:hasExternalReference ?tempExt .
    ?tempExt ref:hasTimeseriesId ?timeseriesId .
    BIND("AHU Supply Air Temperature" AS ?measurement)
  }
  UNION
  {
    # Retrieve the AHU airflow rate sensor timeseries ID
    bldg1:hvac_ahu brick:hasPoint ?airflowPoint .
    ?airflowPoint a brick:Supply_Air_Flow_Sensor .
    ?airflowPoint ref:hasExternalReference ?afExt .
    ?afExt ref:hasTimeseriesId ?timeseriesId .
    BIND("AHU Airflow Rate Reading" AS ?measurement)
  }
  UNION
  {
    # Retrieve the AHU airflow setpoint timeseries ID
    bldg1:hvac_ahu brick:hasPoint ?afSetpoint .
    ?afSetpoint a brick:Supply_Air_Flow_Setpoint .
    ?afSetpoint ref:hasExternalReference ?afSetExt .
    ?afSetExt ref:hasTimeseriesId ?timeseriesId .
    BIND("AHU Airflow Setpoint" AS ?measurement)
  }
}

question: Which Fahrenheit air temperature sensors are installed in core areas of the HVAC domain, and what are the labels of those sensors, their measurement points, and the spaces?
original_sparql:
PREFIX brick: <https://brickschema.org/schema/Brick/>
PREFIX s223: <http://data.ashrae.org/standard223#>
PREFIX qudt: <http://qudt.org/schema/qudt/>
PREFIX unit: <http://qudt.org/vocab/unit/>
PREFIX rdfs: <http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#>

SELECT ?sensor ?sensorLabel ?measurementPointLabel ?spaceLabel
WHERE {
  # Identify Fahrenheit air temperature sensors using the Brick class
  ?sensor a brick:Air_Temperature_Sensor ;
          rdfs:label ?sensorLabel .

  # Get the measurement point via a standard relationship (using Brick's hasPoint or its inverse)
  { ?sensor brick:hasPoint ?mp . }
  UNION
  { ?mp brick:isPointOf ?sensor . }
  ?mp rdfs:label ?measurementPointLabel .

  # Ensure that either the sensor or its measurement point has the Fahrenheit unit defined
  { ?sensor qudt:hasUnit unit:FAHRENHEIT . }
  UNION
  { ?mp qudt:hasUnit unit:FAHRENHEIT . }
  
  # Link the measurement point to a space where it is installed via a standard spatial relation
  ?mp brick:isLocatedIn ?space .
  ?space rdfs:label ?spaceLabel .

  # Filter spaces to ones that are explicitly classified as being part of the HVAC domain (for example, an HVAC zone)
  # Here we assume that spaces in the HVAC domain are typed as brick:HVAC_Zone
  ?space a brick:HVAC_Zone .
}

question: Which time series should I pull to see how each zone and its VAV are performing in terms of temperature control and occupancy?
original_sparql:
PREFIX bldg1: <urn:bldg/>
PREFIX brick: <https://brickschema.org/schema/Brick#>
PREFIX ref: <https://brickschema.org/schema/Brick/ref#>

SELECT ?zone
       (GROUP_CONCAT(DISTINCT ?tempTS; SEPARATOR=", ") AS ?temperatureTimeSeries)
       (GROUP_CONCAT(DISTINCT ?occTS; SEPARATOR=", ") AS ?occupancyTimeSeries)
       (GROUP_CONCAT(DISTINCT ?vavTS; SEPARATOR=", ") AS ?vavTimeSeries)
WHERE {
  ?zone a brick:HVAC_Zone .

  # Temperature-related timeseries for the zone
  OPTIONAL {
    ?zone brick:hasPoint ?tempPoint .
    ?tempPoint a ?tempType .
    FILTER(?tempType IN (brick:Zone_Air_Temperature_Sensor,
                           brick:Zone_Air_Cooling_Temperature_Setpoint,
                           brick:Zone_Air_Heating_Temperature_Setpoint))
    ?tempPoint ref:hasExternalReference ?tempExt .
    ?tempExt ref:hasTimeseriesId ?tempTS .
  }

  # Occupancy sensor timeseries for the zone
  OPTIONAL {
    ?zone brick:hasPoint ?occPoint .
    ?occPoint a brick:Occupancy_Sensor .
    ?occPoint ref:hasExternalReference ?occExt .
    ?occExt ref:hasTimeseriesId ?occTS .
  }

  # VAV equipment timeseries for the zone
  # Here we assume that a zone is linked to its VAV equipment via brick:hasEquipment
  OPTIONAL {
    ?zone brick:hasEquipment ?vav .
    {
      ?vav brick:hasPoint ?vavPoint .
      ?vavPoint a brick:Valve_Command .
      ?vavPoint ref:hasExternalReference ?vavExt .
      ?vavExt ref:hasTimeseriesId ?vavTS .
    } UNION {
      ?vav brick:hasPoint ?vavPoint .
      ?vavPoint a brick:Damper_Position_Setpoint .
      ?vavPoint ref:hasExternalReference ?vavExt .
      ?vavExt ref:hasTimeseriesId ?vavTS .
    }
  }
}
GROUP BY ?zone

question: What is the IFC name of each zone and the timeseries id from the external reference of the occupancy sensor that is located in a space which is part of that zone?
Where can I find the timeseries identifiers for the minimum temperature settings in each building zone and the names of those zones?
original_sparql:
PREFIX brick: <https://brickschema.org/schema/Brick#>
PREFIX ref: <https://brickschema.org/schema/Brick/ref#>
PREFIX rdfs: <http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#>
PREFIX saref: <https://saref.etsi.org/core#>

SELECT DISTINCT (COALESCE(?zoneLabel, ?zoneHasName, STRAFTER(STR(?zone), "#")) AS ?zoneName) ?timeseriesId
WHERE {
  # Select minimum air temperature setpoints and extract their timeseries ids
  ?minSet a brick:Min_Air_Temperature_Setpoint .
  ?minSet ref:hasExternalReference ?minExt .
  ?minExt ref:hasTimeseriesId ?timeseriesId .
  
  # Trace from the min setpoint to the equipment
  ?minSet brick:isPointOf ?equip .
  
  # Find the space associated with the equipment
  { ?equip brick:isLocationOf ?space } UNION { ?equip brick:hasLocation ?space } .
  
  # Associate the space with the zone. In our data model, a zone contains one or more spaces.
  ?zone brick:hasPart ?space .

  # Attempt to retrieve the zone name via common naming properties
  OPTIONAL { ?zone rdfs:label ?zoneLabel . }
  OPTIONAL { ?zone brick:hasName ?zoneHasName . }
}
ORDER BY ?zoneName

question: What alc and mpc endpoints are defined for the read-only properties of each zone?
original_sparql:
PREFIX s223: <http://data.ashrae.org/standard223#>
PREFIX bob: <http://data.ashrae.org/standard223/si-builder#>
PREFIX ref: <https://brickschema.org/schema/Brick/>
PREFIX rdfs: <http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#>

# This query returns for each zone (identified via its zone connection point) and each read‐only
# property (typed as s223:ObservableProperty) associated with that zone the aggregated endpoints.
# Endpoints are distinguished by their ref:source: those matching (case-insensitively) "ALC SOAP API"
# are considered ALC endpoints, while all others (when bound) are considered MPC endpoints.

SELECT ?zone ?property
       (GROUP_CONCAT(DISTINCT IF(bound(?src) && regex(str(?src), "^ALC SOAP API$", "i"), str(?ep), "") ; separator=", ") AS ?alcEndpoints)
       (GROUP_CONCAT(DISTINCT IF(bound(?src) && !regex(str(?src), "^ALC SOAP API$", "i"), str(?ep), "") ; separator=", ") AS ?mpcEndpoints)
WHERE {
  ?zone bob:hasZoneConnectionPoint ?zcp .
  ?property s223:isConnectionPointOf ?zcp .
  ?property a s223:ObservableProperty .
  OPTIONAL {
    ?property s223:hasExternalReference ?ep .
    ?ep ref:source ?src .
  }
}
GROUP BY ?zone ?property
ORDER BY ?zone ?property

question: For each Water-to-Water Heat Pump, what are the related property labels and non-writable quantities' ALC and MPC endpoint references?
How can I retrieve occupancy sensor time series data for every zone in the building?
original_sparql:
PREFIX brick: <https://brickschema.org/schema/Brick#>
PREFIX om: <http://openmetrics.eu/openmetrics#>
PREFIX ref: <https://brickschema.org/schema/Brick/ref#>
PREFIX saref: <https://saref.etsi.org/core#>

# This query retrieves occupancy sensor time series data for each zone,
# explicitly ensuring the sensor is typed as a Brick Occupancy Sensor.
# It navigates from zones to their associated spaces and then to equipment
# that has occupancy sensor points. It then retrieves the external reference
# timeseries identifier and, if available, any associated sensor time series data.

SELECT ?zone ?occSensor ?timeseriesID ?timeSeriesData
WHERE {
  # Identify zones
  ?zone a brick:Zone .
  
  # Navigate from zone to sensor via spaces and equipment. Depending on the model,
  # occupancy sensors may be attached to equipment located in a zone via spaces or directly.
  {
    ?zone brick:hasPart ?space .
    ?space brick:isLocationOf ?equip .
  } UNION {
    ?zone brick:hasPoint ?equip .
  }
  
  # Retrieve occupancy sensor points from the equipment
  ?equip brick:hasPoint ?occSensor .
  
  # Ensure the point is explicitly typed as an occupancy sensor
  ?occSensor a brick:Occupancy_Sensor .
  
  # Verify it observes an occupancy property
  ?occSensor saref:observes om:OccProperty .
  
  # Get the external reference node that contains timeseries mapping
  ?occSensor ref:hasExternalReference ?ext .
  
  # Use OPTIONAL to capture the timeseries identifier
  OPTIONAL { ?ext ref:hasTimeseriesId ?timeseriesID . }
  
  # OPTIONAL: Retrieve the actual time series data if stored in the triple store
  # (This property is assumed in this model; adjust the predicate as necessary.)
  OPTIONAL { ?occSensor brick:hasTimeseriesData ?timeSeriesData . }
}

Which internal properties of water-to-water heat pumps observe specific quantities via non-writable external references, and what are those references’ ALC endpoints and MPC database names along with the property labels?
original_sparql:
PREFIX s223: <http://data.ashrae.org/standard223#>
PREFIX ex1: <http://data.ashrae.org/standard223/data/lbnl-example-2#>
PREFIX ref: <https://brickschema.org/schema/Brick/>
PREFIX qudt: <http://qudt.org/schema/qudt/>
PREFIX rdfs: <http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#>

SELECT DISTINCT ?propertyLabel ?alcEndpoint ?mpcDatabaseName
WHERE {
  # Identify a water-to-water heat pump by selecting equipment that has a water-based medium.
  ?hp a s223:Equipment .
  ?hp s223:hasMedium ?medium .
  FILTER(?medium IN (s223:Water-HotWater, s223:Fluid-Water)) .
  
  # Traverse from the heat pump to one of its internal properties.
  { ?hp s223:hasProperty ?prop . } UNION { ?hp s223:contains ?prop . }
  
  # The internal property must observe a specific physical quantity.
  ?prop qudt:hasQuantityKind ?qkind .
  ?prop rdfs:label ?propertyLabel .
  
  # The property is mapped to an external reference that provides observation information.
  ?prop s223:mapsTo ?ext .
  ?ext a s223:ExternalReference .
  ?ext ref:source ?alcEndpoint .
  
  # Optionally retrieve the MPC database name if it is provided.
  OPTIONAL { ?ext s223:mpcDatabaseName ?mpcDatabaseName } .
  
  # Explicitly ensure that the external reference is non-writable. We assume that if an external
  # reference has a write capability property, then a true value indicates writability. If that property
  # is either missing or false, then the reference is non-writable.
  OPTIONAL { ?ext s223:hasWriteCapability ?wcap . }
  FILTER(!bound(?wcap) || ?wcap = false)
}

question: What are the Zone Air Temperature Sensors and the equipment they belong to?
Find all related sensor and setpoints pairs
original_sparql:
PREFIX brick: <https://brickschema.org/schema/Brick#>
PREFIX rdfs: <http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#>
PREFIX ns2: <http://buildsys.org/ontologies/bldg11#>

# In Brick Schema the association between points and the equipment (or other entities) to which they belong
# is explicitly modeled using properties such as brick:hasPoint (and its inverse, brick:isPointOf).
# A more semantically robust way to identify related sensor–setpoint pairs is to require that they
# are both points on the same equipment. This avoids relying solely on string patterns in IRIs or class names.

SELECT DISTINCT ?sensor ?setpoint
WHERE {
  ?equipment brick:hasPoint ?sensor .
  ?equipment brick:hasPoint ?setpoint .

  # Assert that ?sensor is an instance of a Brick sensor type
  ?sensor a ?sensorClass .
  FILTER(regex(str(?sensorClass), "Sensor$")) .

  # Assert that ?setpoint is an instance of a Brick setpoint type
  ?setpoint a ?setpointClass .
  FILTER(regex(str(?setpointClass), "Setpoint$")) .

  # Optionally, additional domain-specific constraints can be added here if further disambiguation is needed.
}

question: Can you list each zone’s IFC name together with the timeseries ID for its minimum air temperature setpoint?
original_sparql:
PREFIX brick: <https://brickschema.org/schema/Brick#>
PREFIX ref:   <https://brickschema.org/schema/Brick/ref#>
PREFIX rdfs:  <http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#>

SELECT ?ifcName (GROUP_CONCAT(DISTINCT ?tsID; separator=", ") AS ?timeseriesIds)
WHERE {
  # 1) IFC Zones and their names
  ?zone a brick:IfcZone .
  OPTIONAL { ?zone rdfs:label      ?ifcName }
  OPTIONAL { ?zone ref:identifier ?ifcName }

  # 2) Find the Minimum_Air_Temperature_Setpoint on the zone, its spaces, or equipment in those spaces
  ?zone ( brick:hasPoint
         | brick:hasPart/^brick:hasLocation/brick:hasPoint
         ) ?sp .
  ?sp a brick:Minimum_Air_Temperature_Setpoint .

  # 3) Grab the timeseries ID directly on the point
  ?sp ref:timeseriesId ?tsID .
}
GROUP BY ?ifcName

question: For RTU air handling units, what are the property names and their associated ALC and MPC endpoints defined in the external references
Which sensors measuring electric power that reference an external entry in the baseline table have associated timeseries identifiers?
For every device, which measurement instrument and which control value does it include?
original_sparql:
PREFIX brick: <https://brickschema.org/schema/Brick#>
PREFIX rdfs:  <http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#>

SELECT
  ?deviceClass
  (GROUP_CONCAT(DISTINCT STR(?instr); separator=", ")       AS ?instruments)
  (GROUP_CONCAT(DISTINCT STR(?cv);    separator=", ")       AS ?controlValues)
WHERE {
  # find every device class
  ?deviceClass rdfs:subClassOf* brick:Device .

  # follow class‐level hasPoint links
  ?deviceClass brick:hasPoint ?pointClass .

  # optionally classify points as sensors
  OPTIONAL {
    ?pointClass rdfs:subClassOf* brick:Sensor .
    BIND(?pointClass AS ?instr)
  }

  # optionally classify points as control values
  OPTIONAL {
    ?pointClass rdfs:subClassOf* brick:Control_Value .
    BIND(?pointClass AS ?cv)
  }
}
GROUP BY ?deviceClass

  ?zone brick:hasPart+ ?space .

question: Where can I access the read-only operational data endpoints for our water-to-water heat pumps?
original_sparql:
PREFIX s223: <http://data.ashrae.org/standard223#>

  # or its subclasses. The presence of 'hp' in the endpoint string is the only indicator
  # for heat pump related data in the provided example data.
  FILTER (CONTAINS(LCASE(STR(?endpoint)), "hp"))

  # Filter for read-only endpoints: either explicitly marked as "False" or not present
  # (which implies read-only by default for operational data if not explicitly writable).
  OPTIONAL { ?extRef ref:writable ?writableStatus . }
  FILTER ( !BOUND(?writableStatus) || ?writableStatus = "False"^^xsd:boolean )
}

question: For each air handling unit whose label contains rtu, identify any connectable element it contains that observes or has property a property, or any property it has directly, and for each property give its label along with the ALC endpoint and the MPC database name from its external reference

FILTER(CONTAINS(?ahu_label, 'rtu')) ?ahu s223:contains ?element . { ?element s223:observes ?property . } UNION { ?element s223:hasProperty ?property . } ?property rdfs:label ?prop_label . OPTIONAL { ?property s223:hasExternalReference ?ext_ref . ?ext_ref ref:endpoint ?endpoint . ?ext_ref ref:mpc_db_name ?mpc_db_name . } FILTER NOT EXISTS { ?property rdf:type/rdfs:subClassOf* s223:Concept . } FILTER NOT EXISTS { ?property rdf:type/rdfs:subClassOf* s223:Connectable . } }

How can I retrieve occupancy sensor time series data for every zone in the building?

PREFIX brick: <https://brickschema.org/schema/Brick#> 
PREFIX ref: <https://brickschema.org/schema/Brick/ref#>

question: Which air handling unit has point a cooling command and has point a heating command, and what are those points?
original_sparql:
PREFIX brick: <https://brickschema.org/schema/Brick#>
PREFIX rdfs:  <http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#>

SELECT ?ahu
       (GROUP_CONCAT(DISTINCT STR(?cool); separator=", ") AS ?coolCmdURIs)
       (GROUP_CONCAT(DISTINCT ?coolLabel; separator=", ") AS ?coolCmdNames)
       (GROUP_CONCAT(DISTINCT STR(?heat); separator=", ") AS ?heatCmdURIs)
       (GROUP_CONCAT(DISTINCT ?heatLabel; separator=", ") AS ?heatCmdNames)
WHERE {
  ?ahu a brick:Air_Handling_Unit .

  # cooling command points (including any subclasses)
  ?ahu brick:hasPoint ?cool .
  ?cool a/rdfs:subClassOf* brick:Cooling_Command .
  OPTIONAL { ?cool rdfs:label ?coolLabel }

  # heating command points (including any subclasses)
  ?ahu brick:hasPoint ?heat .
  ?heat a/rdfs:subClassOf* brick:Heating_Command .
  OPTIONAL { ?heat rdfs:label ?heatLabel }
}
GROUP BY ?ahu

WHERE {

           brick:hasPart ?space ; 
           ref:hasExternalReference ?zoneRef . 
    ?zoneRef ref:ifcName ?zoneID . 
    ?space brick:isLocationOf ?equipment . 
    ?equipment brick:hasPoint ?point . 
    ?point a brick:Max_Air_Temperature_Setpoint .

    ?pointRef ref:hasTimeseriesId ?timeseriesID . 
    FILTER (CONTAINS(?zoneID, "Zone_")) 
}

question: Which zones map to which data series for their peak temperature settings?
original_sparql:
PREFIX brick: <https://brickschema.org/schema/Brick#>
PREFIX ref: <https://brickschema.org/schema/Brick/ref#>
PREFIX s4bldg: <https://saref.etsi.org/saref4bldg#>

SELECT DISTINCT ?zone ?zoneName ?timeseriesId
WHERE {
  # Get all zones
  ?zone a brick:Zone .
  
  # Optionally get a human‐readable name for the zone
  OPTIONAL {
    ?zone ref:hasExternalReference ?zr .
    ?zr ref:ifcName ?zoneName .
  }
  
  # A zone has one or more spaces as parts
  ?zone brick:hasPart ?space .
  
  # Spaces host equipment (via brick:isLocationOf)
  ?space brick:isLocationOf ?equip .
  
  # Equipment have points; we want the peak (max) temperature setting points
  ?equip brick:hasPoint ?maxPoint .
  ?maxPoint a brick:Max_Air_Temperature_Setpoint .
  
  # Get the data series associated with that point
  ?maxPoint ref:hasExternalReference ?extRef .
  ?extRef ref:hasTimeseriesId ?timeseriesId .
}
ORDER BY ?zone

}

question: List the IFC names of zones alongside their temperature setpoint timeseries IDs
For each Water-to-Water Heat Pump, what are the related non-writable property labels and ALC and MPC endpoint references?
original_sparql:
PREFIX rdfs: <http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#>
PREFIX ref:  <http://data.ashrae.org/standard223/si-builder#>
PREFIX ex1:  <http://data.ashrae.org/standard223/data/lbnl-example-2#>
PREFIX s223: <http://data.ashrae.org/standard223#>

# This query returns, for each Water-to-Water Heat Pump, the labels of related non-writable properties
# along with any ALC (ref:endpoint) and MPC (ref:mpc_db_name) endpoint references.
# It assumes that Water-to-Water Heat Pumps are modeled as instances of ex1:WaterToWaterHeatPump
# and that the pump-to-property relation is modeled via the predicate s223:hasProperty.

SELECT ?pump ?pumpLabel ?propLabel ?alcEndpoint ?mpcEndpoint
WHERE {
  ?pump a ex1:WaterToWaterHeatPump .
  OPTIONAL { ?pump rdfs:label ?pumpLabel . }
  ?pump s223:hasProperty ?prop .
  ?prop rdfs:label ?propLabel .
  ?prop ref:writable ?writable .
  FILTER(?writable = false)
  OPTIONAL { ?prop ref:endpoint ?alcEndpoint . }
  OPTIONAL { ?prop ref:mpc_db_name ?mpcEndpoint . }
}
ORDER BY ?pump ?propLabel

question: Which zones are served by which air handling units via the underfloor air distribution system?
original_sparql:
PREFIX s223: <http://data.ashrae.org/standard223#>
PREFIX ex1: <http://data.ashrae.org/standard223/data/lbnl-example-2#>
PREFIX rdfs: <http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#>

# This query identifies air handling units (AHUs) and zones connected via an intermediate underfloor air distribution terminal.
# Rather than using an overly generic property path, we explicitly require that the terminal component (?ufd) is of one of the known UFAD types.

SELECT DISTINCT ?ahu ?zone
WHERE {
  ?ahu a s223:AirHandlingUnit .
  ?zone a s223:Zone .
  
  # The UFAD terminal component is expected to be typed as one of these candidate types
  VALUES ?ufdType { ex1:UFT ex1:UFT_Fan ex1:UFT_HWC_Valve } .
  ?ufd a ?ufdType .
  
  # Instead of unconstrained recursive traversal, we try to follow explicit connection predicates.
  {  
     ?ahu s223:connectedTo ?ufd .
     ?ufd s223:connectedTo ?zone .
  } UNION {
     ?ahu s223:connectedFrom ?ufd .
     ?ufd s223:connectedFrom ?zone .
  }
}
ORDER BY ?ahu ?zone

question: List each unitary fan terminal along with its volume flow rate or heat flow rate measurements, including each measurement’s name, aspect, recorded value, and unit.

Which properties associated with building zones (including those linked through room domain spaces and chained connections) are read-only, and what are their labels together with the alc and mpc endpoints in their external references?
Which timeseries identifiers correspond to the supply air temperature measurement, supply air flow measurement, and flow setpoint of the AHU supplying air to a space?
original_sparql:
PREFIX bldg1: <urn:bldg/>
PREFIX brick: <https://brickschema.org/schema/Brick#>
PREFIX ref: <https://brickschema.org/schema/Brick/ref#>

SELECT DISTINCT ?tsTemp ?tsFlow ?tsFlowSet
WHERE {
  # Identify any AHU that supplies air indirectly to a space (via a VAV feeding an HVAC_Zone)
  ?ahu a brick:AHU .
  ?ahu brick:feeds ?vav .
  ?vav brick:feeds ?zone .
  ?zone a brick:HVAC_Zone .
  
  # Get the supply air temperature measurement from the AHU
  ?ahu brick:hasPoint ?tempPoint .
  ?tempPoint a brick:Supply_Air_Temperature_Sensor .
  ?tempPoint ref:hasExternalReference ?tempRef .
  ?tempRef ref:hasTimeseriesId ?tsTemp .
  
  # Get the supply air flow measurement from the AHU
  ?ahu brick:hasPoint ?flowPoint .
  ?flowPoint a brick:Supply_Air_Flow_Sensor .
  ?flowPoint ref:hasExternalReference ?flowRef .
  ?flowRef ref:hasTimeseriesId ?tsFlow .
  
  # Get the flow setpoint. In many Brick models the flow is controlled via a damper setpoint on the VAV (linked to air flow control).
  ?vav brick:hasPoint ?setPoint .
  ?setPoint a brick:Damper_Position_Setpoint .
  ?setPoint ref:hasExternalReference ?setRef .
  ?setRef ref:hasTimeseriesId ?tsFlowSet .
}

question: Which underfloor distribution devices serve which thermal zones, and from which air handling units do they receive air?
Find all sensor and setpoints connected to the same equipment
original_sparql:
PREFIX brick: <https://brickschema.org/schema/Brick#>
PREFIX rdfs: <http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#>

SELECT DISTINCT ?equipment ?sensor ?setpoint
WHERE {
  ?equipment a brick:Equipment .
  ?equipment brick:hasPoint ?sensor .
  ?sensor a ?sensorType .
  ?sensorType rdfs:subClassOf* brick:Sensor .

  ?equipment brick:hasPoint ?setpoint .
  ?setpoint a ?setpointType .
  ?setpointType rdfs:subClassOf* brick:Setpoint .
}
ORDER BY ?equipment ?sensor ?setpoint

question: Which equipment has a zone air temperature sensor and a zone air temperature setpoint, and what are the names of those sensor and setpoint points?
How do I access the supply air measurement data for an AHU?
For each zone, what is the timeseries ID of its minimum air temperature setpoint, and what is the zone’s IFC reference?
original_sparql:
PREFIX brick: <https://brickschema.org/schema/Brick#>
PREFIX ref: <https://brickschema.org/schema/Brick/ref#>

SELECT DISTINCT ?zoneIFCName ?minTSID
WHERE {
  # Each zone with its IFC reference
  ?zone a brick:Zone ;
        ref:hasExternalReference ?zoneIFC .
  ?zoneIFC ref:ifcName ?zoneIFCName .

  # Find a space that is part of the zone
  ?zone brick:hasPart ?space .

  # A space in turn hosts some equipment
  ?space brick:isLocationOf ?equip .

  # Equipment have points; we filter to the minimum air temperature setpoints
  ?equip brick:hasPoint ?minSetpoint .
  ?minSetpoint a brick:Min_Air_Temperature_Setpoint ;
               ref:hasExternalReference ?tsRef .

  # Get the timeseries id from the external reference of the setpoint
  ?tsRef ref:hasTimeseriesId ?minTSID .
}
ORDER BY ?zoneIFCName

question: What are the labels of the sensors that observe a quantifiable observable property of temperature with unit degrees Fahrenheit on fluid air, and what are the labels of those temperature properties and the domain spaces (with domain HVAC and defined as core spaces) where those sensors have their observation location?
original_sparql:
PREFIX brick: <https://brickschema.org/schema/Brick#>
PREFIX qudt: <http://qudt.org/schema/qudt/>
PREFIX unit: <http://qudt.org/vocab/unit/>
PREFIX rdfs: <http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#>

# This query searches for sensors (Brick:Sensor) that observe a property which is intended to represent a temperature measurement.
# We restrict the observed property by ensuring its label contains the string "temperature" (in lowercase) and that its unit is degrees Fahrenheit.
# We also require the property to be associated with the medium Fluid Air (using Brick's naming convention, adjust if needed).
# For each sensor, we follow its observation location via brick:hasLocation, and we require that location is modeled as a core space
# (here assumed to be of type brick:CoreSpace) and that the location is linked to a domain whose label contains "hvac" (ignoring case).

SELECT ?sensorLabel ?tempPropLabel ?locationLabel
WHERE {
  ?sensor a brick:Sensor .
  ?sensor brick:observes ?tempProp .

  # Ensure the observed property is temperature-related by checking its label contains "temperature"
  ?tempProp rdfs:label ?tempPropLabel .
  FILTER(CONTAINS(LCASE(?tempPropLabel), "temperature"))

  # The property must be quantified in degrees Fahrenheit
  ?tempProp qudt:hasUnit unit:DEG_F .

  # Constrain the medium to Fluid Air. (Adjust the predicate and URI if your dataset uses a different convention.)
  ?tempProp brick:hasMedium brick:Fluid_Air .

  # Get the sensor's observation location using the Brick convention
  ?sensor brick:hasLocation ?location .

  # The location should be a core space. Depending on your implementation, this might be a different class; adjust as needed.
  ?location a brick:CoreSpace .

  # Also, the location should have an associated domain with label containing "hvac".
  ?location brick:hasDomain ?domain .
  ?domain rdfs:label ?domainLabel .
  FILTER(CONTAINS(LCASE(?domainLabel), "hvac"))

  OPTIONAL { ?sensor rdfs:label ?sensorLabel . }
  OPTIONAL { ?location rdfs:label ?locationLabel . }
}
ORDER BY ?sensorLabel ?tempPropLabel ?locationLabel

question: Where can I find the recorded series for electricity consumption and heating power?
How are room temperature measurements and their target values defined across the building’s equipment?
original_sparql:
PREFIX brick: <https://brickschema.org/schema/Brick#>
PREFIX rdfs: <http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#>

# This query attempts to capture how room/zone temperature measurements and their
# associated target setpoints are defined across building equipment. Instead of
# limiting the equipment by a specific class, we retrieve any equipment that has points
# whose type names include "Temperature_Sensor" (for measurements) and whose setpoint types
# include "Temperature_Setpoint". This uses REGEX filtering to be more inclusive of subclass
# variations and does not over constrain the equipment types (e.g., Room, Zone, etc.).

SELECT DISTINCT ?equipment ?measurement ?measType (GROUP_CONCAT(DISTINCT CONCAT(str(?target), " (", str(?targetType), ")") ; separator = ", ") AS ?targetInfo)
WHERE {
  ?equipment brick:hasPoint ?measurement .
  ?measurement a ?measType .
  FILTER(regex(str(?measType), "Temperature_Sensor", "i"))

  OPTIONAL {
    ?equipment brick:hasPoint ?target .
    ?target a ?targetType .
    FILTER(regex(str(?targetType), "Temperature_Setpoint", "i"))
  }
}
GROUP BY ?equipment ?measurement ?measType
ORDER BY ?equipment ?measurement

What volume or heat flow rate properties does each UFT have, including their labels, aspects, values, and units?

For each air handling unit whose label contains rtu, identify any connectable element it contains that observes or has property a property, or any property it has directly, and for each property give its label along with the ALC endpoint and the MPC database name from its external reference
original_sparql:
PREFIX bacnet: <http://data.ashrae.org/bacnet/2020#>
PREFIX bob: <http://data.ashrae.org/standard223/si-builder#>
PREFIX ex1: <http://data.ashrae.org/standard223/data/lbnl-example-2#>
PREFIX owl: <http://www.w3.org/2002/07/owl#>
PREFIX quantitykind: <http://qudt.org/vocab/quantitykind/>
PREFIX qudt: <http://qudt.org/schema/qudt/>
PREFIX rdf: <http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#>
PREFIX rdfs: <http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#>
PREFIX ref: <https://brickschema.org/schema/Brick/>
PREFIX s223: <http://data.ashrae.org/standard223#>
PREFIX sh: <http://www.w3.org/ns/shacl#>
PREFIX unit: <http://qudt.org/vocab/unit/>
PREFIX xsd: <http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#>

SELECT ?ahu ?ahuLabel ?propertySource ?propertySourceLabel ?property ?propertyLabel ?alcEndpoint ?mpcDatabaseName
WHERE {
    ?ahu a ref:Air_Handling_Unit .
    ?ahu rdfs:label ?ahuLabel .
    FILTER regex(str(?ahuLabel), "rtu", "i") .

    {
        # Case 1a: Property of a contained connectable element (via hasProperty)
        ?ahu s223:contains ?propertySource .
        ?propertySource a s223:ConnectableElement .
        ?propertySource rdfs:label ?propertySourceLabel .
        ?propertySource s223:hasProperty ?property .
    } UNION {
        # Case 1b: Property observed by a Point contained within a connectable element
        # The 'propertySource' here is the ConnectableElement that contains the observing Point.
        ?ahu s223:contains ?propertySource .
        ?propertySource a s223:ConnectableElement .
        ?propertySource rdfs:label ?propertySourceLabel .
        ?propertySource s223:contains ?point .
        ?point a s223:Point .
        ?point s223:observes ?property .
    } UNION {
        # Case 2: Property of the AHU directly
        BIND(?ahu AS ?propertySource) .
        BIND(?ahuLabel AS ?propertySourceLabel) .
        ?ahu s223:hasProperty ?property .
    }

    ?property rdfs:label ?propertyLabel .
    OPTIONAL {
        ?property s223:hasExternalReference ?extRef .
        ?extRef s223:hasALCEndpoint ?alcEndpoint .
        ?extRef s223:hasMPCDatabaseName ?mpcDatabaseName .
    }
}

question: Which zones map to which data series for their peak temperature settings?
Can you list all sensors monitoring air temperature in Fahrenheit within core HVAC areas along with their measurement point names and the names of their locations?
original_sparql:
PREFIX s223: <http://data.ashrae.org/standard223#>
PREFIX rdfs: <http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#>
PREFIX qudt: <http://qudt.org/schema/qudt/>
PREFIX unit: <http://qudt.org/vocab/unit/>

# This query looks for sensors that (a) are temperature sensors monitoring air, (b) have their measurement points reporting in Fahrenheit, and
# (c) are associated with locations that represent core (or central) HVAC areas. In some cases the unit info is attached at the measurement
# point level, in others directly on the sensor if no measurement point is explicitly defined.

SELECT ?sensor ?mp ?measurementPointName ?location ?locationName
WHERE {
  # Identify air temperature sensors. (Adjust this class as needed if the dataset uses an alternate type.)
  ?sensor a s223:TemperatureSensor .
  
  # Get the sensor's observation location
  ?sensor s223:hasObservationLocation ?location .
  ?location rdfs:label ?locationName .
  
  # Instead of a simple regex on locationName, a more robust check might involve a specific type or tag. For now, we assume that
  # locations whose labels contain 'core' or 'central' indicate core HVAC areas.
  FILTER(regex(?locationName, "core|central", "i"))
  
  # Retrieve the measurement unit info. In many schemas, the measurement point will carry the unit property. In cases where the sensor
  # does not have an explicit measurement point, we use the sensor itself.
  {
    ?sensor s223:hasMeasurementPoint ?mp .
    ?mp rdfs:label ?measurementPointName .
    ?mp qudt:hasUnit unit:DEG_F .
  } UNION {
    FILTER NOT EXISTS { ?sensor s223:hasMeasurementPoint ?anyMP }.
    ?sensor rdfs:label ?measurementPointName .
    ?sensor qudt:hasUnit unit:DEG_F .
    BIND(?sensor AS ?mp)
  }
}
ORDER BY ?locationName ?measurementPointName

PREFIX sh: <http://www.w3.org/ns/shacl#>
PREFIX unit: <http://qudt.org/vocab/unit/>
PREFIX xsd: <http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#>

SELECT ?rtu ?property ?endpoint
WHERE {
    ?rtu a ref:RTU .
    ?property s223:isPropertyOf ?rtu .
    ?endpoint s223:isEndpointOf ?property .

    # Ensure it's an external endpoint
    ?endpoint s223:externalEndpoint true .

    # Ensure it's nonwritable
    ?endpoint s223:writable false .

    # Ensure it's either an ALC or MPC endpoint
    { ?endpoint a s223:ALCEndpoint . }
    UNION
    { ?endpoint a s223:MPCEndpoint . }
}

question: Where can I find the timeseries IDs for an air handling unit’s supply-side measurements?

PREFIX bldg1: <urn:bldg/>

PREFIX ref: <https://brickschema.org/schema/Brick/ref#>
PREFIX xsd: <http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#>

SELECT DISTINCT ?timeseriesId
WHERE {
    ?ahu a brick:Air_Handling_Unit .
    { # Find points directly on the AHU
        ?ahu brick:hasPoint ?point .
    }
    UNION
    { # Find points on parts of the AHU
        ?ahu brick:hasPart ?part .
        ?part brick:hasPoint ?point .
    }

    { # Condition 1: Point is directly classified as a Supply_Air_Sensor
        ?point a ?sensorType .
        ?sensorType rdfs:subClassOf* brick:Supply_Air_Sensor .
    }
    UNION
    { # Condition 2: Point is a Sensor and located in a Supply_Air_System
        ?point a ?genericSensorType .
        ?genericSensorType rdfs:subClassOf* brick:Sensor .
        ?point brick:hasLocation ?supplySystem .
        ?supplySystem a brick:Supply_Air_System .
    }
    ?point ref:hasTimeseriesId ?timeseriesId .
}

question: For each Water-to-Water Heat Pump, what are the related non-writable property labels and ALC and MPC endpoint references?
original_sparql:
PREFIX bacnet: <http://data.ashrae.org/bacnet/2020#>
PREFIX bob: <http://data.ashrae.org/standard223/si-builder#>
PREFIX ex1: <http://data.ashrae.org/standard223/data/lbnl-example-2#>
PREFIX owl: <http://www.w3.org/2002/07/owl#>
PREFIX quantitykind: <http://qudt.org/vocab/quantitykind/>
PREFIX qudt: <http://qudt.org/schema/qudt/>
PREFIX rdf: <http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#>
PREFIX rdfs: <http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#>
PREFIX ref: <https://brickschema.org/schema/Brick/>
PREFIX s223: <http://data.ashrae.org/standard223#>
PREFIX sh: <http://www.w3.org/ns/shacl#>
PREFIX unit: <http://qudt.org/vocab/unit/>
PREFIX xsd: <http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#>

SELECT ?heatPump
       (GROUP_CONCAT(DISTINCT ?propertyLabel; SEPARATOR=", ") AS ?nonWritablePropertyLabels)
       (GROUP_CONCAT(DISTINCT ?alcEndpoint; SEPARATOR=", ") AS ?alcEndpointReferences)
       (GROUP_CONCAT(DISTINCT ?mpcEndpoint; SEPARATOR=", ") AS ?mpcEndpointReferences)
WHERE {
    ?heatPump a ref:Water_to_Water_Heat_Pump .

    # Find candidate s223 entities related to the heat pump.
    # These can be the heat pump itself, or an associated s223:Device/Component.
    {
        BIND(?heatPump AS ?candidate_s223_entity) .
    }
    UNION
    {
        ?heatPump s223:hasDevice ?candidate_s223_entity .
    }
    UNION
    {
        ?heatPump s223:hasComponent ?candidate_s223_entity .
    }

    # Ensure the candidate entity is actually an s223:Device or s223:Component
    # as these are the types that have s223:hasProperty and s223:hasEndpoint.
    ?candidate_s223_entity a ?s223_type .
    FILTER (?s223_type = s223:Device || ?s223_type = s223:Component) .
    BIND(?candidate_s223_entity AS ?s223_entity) .

    OPTIONAL {
        ?s223_entity s223:hasProperty ?property .
        ?property s223:isWritable false .
        ?property rdfs:label ?propertyLabel .
    }

    OPTIONAL {
        ?s223_entity s223:hasEndpoint ?alcEndpoint .
        ?alcEndpoint a s223:Analog_Command_Endpoint .
    }

    OPTIONAL {
        ?s223_entity s223:hasEndpoint ?mpcEndpoint .
        ?mpcEndpoint a s223:Multi_State_Command_Endpoint .
    }
} 
GROUP BY ?heatPump

question: Find all Building electrical meters and outside air temperature sensors in my building
original_sparql:
PREFIX brick: <https://brickschema.org/schema/Brick#>
PREFIX ex: <http://example.com#>
PREFIX rdfs: <http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#>

# This query attempts to retrieve two sets of devices within a given building (ex:MyBuilding):
# 1. Building electrical meters. We match instances that are or inherit from brick:Electric_Meter.
# 2. Outside air temperature sensors. Since Brick datasets often model these as subclasses of brick:Temperature_Sensor,
#    we match instances that are in the Temperature_Sensor hierarchy and then filter by a tag that indicates they monitor
#    outdoor (for example, a tag with the literal 'Outdoor' in its IRI or label). 
# 
# Devices are generally linked to a location via brick:hasLocation. Locations are then associated with the overall
# building via a chain of containment relationships (brick:isPartOf or direct equality). The UNION in the location
# pattern covers both cases: directly on the building or nested within parts of the building.

SELECT DISTINCT ?device ?deviceType
WHERE {
  {
    # Select Building electrical meters - using subclass matching in case the meter is a subclass
    ?device a/rdfs:subClassOf* brick:Electric_Meter .
    ?device brick:hasLocation ?loc .
    { ?loc brick:isPartOf+ ex:MyBuilding . } UNION { ?loc = ex:MyBuilding . }
    BIND("Building Electrical Meter" AS ?deviceType)
  } UNION {
    # Select Outside air temperature sensors
    # We look for devices that are (or subclass of) Temperature_Sensor and which have a tag indicating an outdoor sensor.
    ?device a/rdfs:subClassOf* brick:Temperature_Sensor .
    ?device brick:hasTag ?tag .
    # The following FILTER assumes that tags for outdoor sensors contain the string "Outdoor" (case insensitive).
    FILTER(regex(str(?tag), "Outdoor", "i")) .
    ?device brick:hasLocation ?loc .
    { ?loc brick:isPartOf+ ex:MyBuilding . } UNION { ?loc = ex:MyBuilding . }
    BIND("Outside Air Temperature Sensor" AS ?deviceType)
  }
}

question: How can I find the timeseries IDs for power measurements stored under the flexible_ table?
Identify each entity that is a zone, and either has a directly associated property or is indirectly connected to a property via a RoomDomainSpace through a chain of connections.

PREFIX bacnet: <http://data.ashrae.org/bacnet/2020#>
PREFIX bob: <http://data.ashrae.org/standard223/si-builder#>
PREFIX ex1: <http://data.ashrae.org/standard223/data/lbnl-example-2#>
PREFIX owl: <http://www.w3.org/2002/07/owl#>
PREFIX quantitykind: <http://qudt.org/vocab/quantitykind/>
PREFIX qudt: <http://qudt.org/schema/qudt/>
PREFIX rdf: <http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#>
PREFIX rdfs: <http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#>
PREFIX ref: <https://brickschema.org/schema/Brick/>
PREFIX s223: <http://data.ashrae.org/standard223#>
PREFIX sh: <http://www.w3.org/ns/shacl#>
PREFIX unit: <http://qudt.org/vocab/unit/>
PREFIX xsd: <http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#>

SELECT DISTINCT ?zone ?property
WHERE {
    ?zone rdf:type s223:Zone .
    {
        # Direct connection to a property
        ?zone s223:hasProperty ?property .
    }
    UNION
    {
        # Indirect connection to a property via a RoomDomainSpace
        ?zone s223:hasRoomDomainSpace ?roomDomainSpace .
        ?roomDomainSpace s223:hasProperty ?property .
    }
}

question: How big are the buildings by their area measurements?
Show me all available power measurement series.
What is the IFC name of each zone and the timeseries id from the external reference of the occupancy sensor that is located in a space which is part of that zone?
original_sparql:
PREFIX brick: <https://brickschema.org/schema/Brick#>
PREFIX rdfs: <http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#>
PREFIX sh: <http://www.w3.org/ns/shacl#>
PREFIX sdo: <http://schema.org/>
PREFIX unit: <http://qudt.org/vocab/unit/>
PREFIX xsd: <http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#>
PREFIX ashrae: <https://w3id.org/ashrae#>
PREFIX ifc: <http://standards.buildingsmart.org/IFC/DEV/IFC4/ADD2/OWL#>

SELECT DISTINCT ?zoneIfcName ?zoneLabel ?timeseriesId
WHERE {
    ?zone a brick:Zone .
    
    OPTIONAL { ?zone ifc:name ?zoneIfcName . } # Directly get IFC name if available
    OPTIONAL { ?zone rdfs:label ?rdfsLabel . }
    OPTIONAL { ?zone sdo:name ?sdoName . }

    # Provide a general label as a fallback if a specific IFC name is not present
    BIND(COALESCE(?rdfsLabel, ?sdoName) AS ?zoneLabel) .

    ?zone brick:hasPart ?space .
    ?occupancySensor a brick:Occupancy_Sensor .
    ?occupancySensor brick:hasLocation ?space .
    ?occupancySensor brick:hasExternalReference ?externalRef .
    ?externalRef brick:timeseriesId ?timeseriesId .

    # Ensure at least one type of name (IFC or general label) is found for the zone
    FILTER(BOUND(?zoneIfcName) || BOUND(?zoneLabel))
}

question: Where can I find the read-only control and database addresses for the attributes of water-to-water heat pumps?
What are all of the monitoring and control data streams temperature, setpoints, occupancy and VAV controls available for each zone in the system?

PREFIX bldg1: <urn:bldg/>
PREFIX brick: <https://brickschema.org/schema/Brick#>
PREFIX rdfs: <http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#>
PREFIX ref: <https://brickschema.org/schema/Brick/ref#>
PREFIX xsd: <http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#>

SELECT DISTINCT ?zone ?point ?point_type WHERE {
  {
    ?zone a brick:Zone .
    ?point brick:isPointOf ?zone .
    ?point a ?point_type .
    FILTER (
      ?point_type = brick:Occupancy_Sensor ||
      ?point_type rdfs:subClassOf* brick:Temperature_Sensor ||
      ?point_type rdfs:subClassOf* brick:Setpoint
    )
  }
  UNION
  {
    ?zone a brick:Zone .
    ?equipment brick:locatedIn ?zone .
    ?equipment brick:hasPoint ?point .
    ?point a ?point_type .
    FILTER (
      ?point_type = brick:Occupancy_Sensor ||
      ?point_type rdfs:subClassOf* brick:Temperature_Sensor ||
      ?point_type rdfs:subClassOf* brick:Setpoint
    )
  }
  UNION
  {
    ?zone a brick:Zone .
    ?vav_box a brick:VAV .
    ?vav_box brick:feeds ?zone .
    ?vav_box brick:hasPoint ?point .
    ?point a ?point_type .
    FILTER (
      ?point_type = brick:VAV_Damper_Position_Sensor ||
      ?point_type = brick:VAV_Damper_Position_Setpoint ||
      ?point_type = brick:VAV_Flow_Sensor ||
      ?point_type = brick:VAV_Flow_Setpoint
    )
  }
}

question: Can you list every zone’s name along with the identifier of the timeseries that records its highest temperature setpoint?
What point entities exist and what types are they assigned?
What are all of the monitoring and control data streams temperature, setpoints, occupancy and VAV controls available for each zone in the system?
Where can I find the timeseries IDs for an air handling unit’s supply-side measurements?
original_sparql:
PREFIX bldg1: <urn:bldg/>
PREFIX brick: <https://brickschema.org/schema/Brick#>
PREFIX rdfs: <http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#>
PREFIX ref: <https://brickschema.org/schema/Brick/ref#>
PREFIX xsd: <http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#>

SELECT DISTINCT ?ahu ?point ?pointType ?timeseries_id
WHERE {
  ?ahu a brick:AirHandlingUnit .
  
  # Allow for two modeling approaches:
  # 1. The supply-side measurement point is directly attached by brick:hasPoint
  # 2. It is attached via an intermediate supply branch using brick:feeds/brick:hasPoint
  ?ahu (brick:hasPoint | brick:feeds/brick:hasPoint) ?point .
  
  ?point brick:hasTimeseries ?ts .
  ?ts rdfs:label ?timeseries_id .
  
  # Get the type(s) associated with the point
  ?point a ?pointType .
  
  # Filter to supply-side measurement points by checking if the type IRI contains 'Supply'
  FILTER(CONTAINS(STR(?pointType), "Supply"))
}

SELECT DISTINCT ?sensor ?tempPointLabel ?hvacLabel
WHERE {
  # Identify sensors (using Brick modeling conventions)
  ?sensor rdf:type ref:Sensor .
  
  # Link sensor to its measurement point using either of the known predicates
  { ?sensor ref:hasPoint ?tempPoint . } UNION { ?sensor ref:hasMeasurementPoint ?tempPoint . }
  
  # The measurement point should represent air temperature. Allow for alternate typing.
  { ?tempPoint rdf:type ref:Air_Temperature . } UNION { ?tempPoint rdf:type ref:Temperature_Sensor . }
  
  # Check that the measurement uses degrees Fahrenheit. We allow for either a direct QUDT unit reference or a literal value.
  { ?tempPoint qudt:unit unit:FAHR . } UNION { 
      ?tempPoint qudt:unit ?unitVal .
      FILTER (lcase(str(?unitVal)) = "fahr" || str(?unitVal) = "°F")
  }
  
  # Retrieve the human-readable label for the temperature measurement point
  ?tempPoint rdfs:label ?tempPointLabel .
  
  # Determine the location of the sensor. The HVAC core space may be linked at the sensor or measurement point level.
  { ?sensor ref:isLocatedIn ?space . } UNION { ?tempPoint ref:isLocatedIn ?space . }
  
  # Ensure the located space is an HVAC core space
  ?space rdf:type ref:HVAC_Core_Space .
  ?space rdfs:label ?hvacLabel .
}

question: What are the labels of the sensors that observe a quantifiable observable property of temperature with unit degrees Fahrenheit on fluid air, and what are the labels of those temperature properties and the domain spaces (with domain HVAC and defined as core spaces) where those sensors have their observation location?
original_sparql:
PREFIX bacnet: <http://data.ashrae.org/bacnet/2020#>
PREFIX bob: <http://data.ashrae.org/standard223/si-builder#>
PREFIX ex1: <http://data.ashrae.org/standard223/data/lbnl-example-2#>
PREFIX owl: <http://www.w3.org/2002/07/owl#>
PREFIX quantitykind: <http://qudt.org/vocab/quantitykind/>
PREFIX qudt: <http://qudt.org/schema/qudt/>
PREFIX rdf: <http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#>
PREFIX rdfs: <http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#>
PREFIX ref: <https://brickschema.org/schema/Brick/>
PREFIX s223: <http://data.ashrae.org/standard223#>
PREFIX sh: <http://www.w3.org/ns/shacl#>
PREFIX unit: <http://qudt.org/vocab/unit/>
PREFIX xsd: <http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#>

# This query finds sensors (allowing for either s223:Sensor or ref:Sensor) that observe a quantifiable
# observable property of temperature. The property is identified by its type (s223:QuantifiableObservableProperty)
# and by having a specific quantity kind (quantitykind:Temperature) and unit (unit:degF). Moreover, the property
# applies to fluid air (ex1:FluidAir). The sensor's observation location is then required to be a domain space
# that is typed as s223:CoreSpace and is assigned to the HVAC domain (s223:HVAC). The query returns the labels of the
# sensor, the temperature property, and the domain space.

SELECT DISTINCT ?sensorLabel ?tempPropertyLabel ?domainLabel
WHERE {
  # Allow for sensor to be typed under either s223 or Brick
  { ?sensor a s223:Sensor. } UNION { ?sensor a ref:Sensor. }
  ?sensor rdfs:label ?sensorLabel .

  # Sensor observes a temperature property using a property predicate that may come from s223 or bob
  ?sensor (s223:observes | bob:observes) ?tempProperty .

  # The observed property must be a quantifiable observable property of temperature
  ?tempProperty a s223:QuantifiableObservableProperty .
  ?tempProperty s223:hasQuantityKind quantitykind:Temperature .

  # The property uses a known QUDT unit for degrees Fahrenheit
  ?tempProperty qudt:unit unit:degF .

  # The property applies to Fluid Air
  ?tempProperty s223:appliesTo ex1:FluidAir .

  # Retrieve the label for the temperature property
  ?tempProperty rdfs:label ?tempPropertyLabel .

  # Get the sensor's observation location, permitting alternative property names
  ?sensor (ref:hasLocation | s223:hasLocation) ?domainSpace .
  ?domainSpace rdfs:label ?domainLabel .

  # The domain space must be explicitly defined as a core space
  ?domainSpace a s223:CoreSpace .

  # And the domain space must be associated with the HVAC domain
  ?domainSpace s223:hasDomain s223:HVAC .
}

question: What read-only control endpoints are available for properties of RTU units
original_sparql:
PREFIX bacnet: <http://data.ashrae.org/bacnet/2020#>
PREFIX bob: <http://data.ashrae.org/standard223/si-builder#>
PREFIX ex1: <http://data.ashrae.org/standard223/data/lbnl-example-2#>
PREFIX owl: <http://www.w3.org/2002/07/owl#>
PREFIX quantitykind: <http://qudt.org/vocab/quantitykind/>
PREFIX qudt: <http://qudt.org/schema/qudt/>
PREFIX rdf: <http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#>
PREFIX rdfs: <http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#>
PREFIX ref: <https://brickschema.org/schema/Brick/>
PREFIX s223: <http://data.ashrae.org/standard223#>
PREFIX sh: <http://www.w3.org/ns/shacl#>
PREFIX unit: <http://qudt.org/vocab/unit/>
PREFIX xsd: <http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#>

SELECT DISTINCT ?endpoint
WHERE {
  # Identify all RTU units using the Brick RTU class
  ?rtu a ref:RTU .
  
  # Connect the RTU to its related points using the Brick hasPoint relationship
  ?rtu ref:hasPoint ?point .
  
  # Ensure the point represents a property by matching typical control point types (if needed, extend this pattern)
  # For instance, you might check if the point is a sensor or actuator; here we assume all points are valid.
  
  # Retrieve the control endpoint associated with the point
  ?point s223:hasControlEndpoint ?endpoint .
  
  # Retrieve the access mode associated with the endpoint
  ?endpoint s223:accessMode ?accessMode .
  
  # Use a case-insensitive regex to match typical representations of read-only access mode (e.g., readOnly, read-only, read only, etc.)
  FILTER(regex(lcase(str(?accessMode)), "^read[- ]?only$"))
}

PREFIX s223: <http://data.ashrae.org/standard223#>
PREFIX sh: <http://www.w3.org/ns/shacl#>
PREFIX unit: <http://qudt.org/vocab/unit/>
PREFIX xsd: <http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#>

# This query retrieves water-to-water heat pumps and, via Brick's standard hasPoint relationship,
# attempts to obtain the associated read-only control and database addresses. In many ASHRAE 223p
# implementations (via the bob namespace) point-level properties store these addresses. Adjust the
# IRIs as needed if your dataset uses alternate naming conventions.

SELECT DISTINCT ?heatPump ?point ?readOnlyControlAddress ?databaseAddress
WHERE {
  # Identify water-to-water heat pumps using the Brick Schema naming convention (CamelCase).
  ?heatPump a ref:WaterToWaterHeatPump .
  
  # Get the associated points (which may represent attributes) -- many Brick-based datasets use hasPoint.
  ?heatPump ref:hasPoint ?point .
  
  # Attempt to retrieve the read-only control address and database address from the point; these are
  # provided via the bob namespace as per ASHRAE 223p implementations.
  OPTIONAL { ?point bob:readOnlyControlAddress ?readOnlyControlAddress . }
  OPTIONAL { ?point bob:databaseAddress ?databaseAddress . }
}
ORDER BY ?heatPump ?point

PREFIX xsd: <http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#>

SELECT DISTINCT ?device ?point
WHERE {
  # Get point from device via either brick:hasSetpoint or brick:hasPoint
  ?device (brick:hasSetpoint|brick:hasPoint) ?point .

  # Ensure the point is associated with air temperature setpoint functionality
  {
    ?point a ?ptType .
    ?ptType rdfs:subClassOf* brick:Air_Temperature_Setpoint .
  }

  # Link the point to a Zone using a property path that covers both directions
  ?point (brick:isPointOf|^brick:hasPoint) ?zone .
  ?zone a brick:Zone .
}

question: For each water-to-water heat pump, what property does it have, what is the property's label, and what are the ALC endpoint and MPC database endpoint of its non-writable external reference?
original_sparql:
PREFIX bacnet: <http://data.ashrae.org/bacnet/2020#>
PREFIX bob: <http://data.ashrae.org/standard223/si-builder#>
PREFIX ex1: <http://data.ashrae.org/standard223/data/lbnl-example-2#>
PREFIX owl: <http://www.w3.org/2002/07/owl#>

PREFIX qudt: <http://qudt.org/schema/qudt/>
PREFIX rdf: <http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#>
PREFIX rdfs: <http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#>
PREFIX ref: <https://brickschema.org/schema/Brick/>
PREFIX s223: <http://data.ashrae.org/standard223#>
PREFIX sh: <http://www.w3.org/ns/shacl#>
PREFIX unit: <http://qudt.org/vocab/unit/>
PREFIX xsd: <http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#>

SELECT ?heatPump ?property ?propertyLabel ?alcEndpoint ?mpcEndpoint
WHERE {
  # Select water-to-water heat pumps using the Brick type
  ?heatPump a ref:WaterToWaterHeatPump .
  
  # Link each heat pump to an intermediate property (point) node via the Brick relationship
  ?heatPump ref:hasPoint ?property .
  
  # The property/point node carries an external reference
  ?property s223:hasExternalReference ?extRef .
  
  # Filter for non-writable external references (explicit boolean literal)
  ?extRef s223:writable "false"^^xsd:boolean ;
          s223:alcEndpoint ?alcEndpoint ;
          s223:mpcDatabaseEndpoint ?mpcEndpoint .
  
  # Optionally, get the label for the property
  OPTIONAL { ?property rdfs:label ?propertyLabel }
}
ORDER BY ?heatPump ?property

question: For each Water-to-Water Heat Pump, what are the related non-writable property labels and ALC and MPC endpoint references?
original_sparql:
PREFIX bacnet: <http://data.ashrae.org/bacnet/2020#>
PREFIX bob: <http://data.ashrae.org/standard223/si-builder#>
PREFIX ex1: <http://data.ashrae.org/standard223/data/lbnl-example-2#>
PREFIX owl: <http://www.w3.org/2002/07/owl#>
PREFIX quantitykind: <http://qudt.org/vocab/quantitykind/>
PREFIX qudt: <http://qudt.org/schema/qudt/>
PREFIX rdf: <http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#>
PREFIX rdfs: <http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#>
PREFIX ref: <https://brickschema.org/schema/Brick/>
PREFIX s223: <http://data.ashrae.org/standard223#>
PREFIX sh: <http://www.w3.org/ns/shacl#>
PREFIX unit: <http://qudt.org/vocab/unit/>
PREFIX xsd: <http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#>

SELECT DISTINCT ?pump ?pumpLabel ?propLabel ?alcEndpoint ?mpcEndpoint
WHERE {
  # Accept either naming variation for the Water-to-Water Heat Pump class
  { ?pump a ref:Water_to_Water_Heat_Pump. }
  UNION
  { ?pump a ref:WaterToWaterHeatPump. }

  OPTIONAL { ?pump rdfs:label ?pumpLabel. }

  # Retrieve associated points. Note: adjust the relationship if needed
  ?pump ref:hasPoint ?point .
  OPTIONAL { ?point rdfs:label ?propLabel. }

  # Optionally bind the writable flag
  OPTIONAL { ?point bob:writable ?writable. }
  # Filter for non-writable properties. Accounts for boolean false or string "false"
  FILTER (!bound(?writable) || (?writable = false || ?writable = "false" || str(?writable) = "false"))
  
  # Optionally get ALC and MPC endpoint references.
  OPTIONAL { ?point bob:ALCEndpoint ?alcEndpoint. }
  OPTIONAL { ?point bob:MPCEndpoint ?mpcEndpoint. }
}

question: For every device, which measurement instrument and which control value does it include?
original_sparql:
PREFIX bacnet: <http://data.ashrae.org/bacnet/2020#>
PREFIX brick: <https://brickschema.org/schema/Brick#>
PREFIX bsh: <https://brickschema.org/schema/BrickShape#>
PREFIX dcterms: <http://purl.org/dc/terms/>
PREFIX ns2: <http://buildsys.org/ontologies/bldg11#>
PREFIX ns3: <http://buildsys.org/ontologies/bldg11#bldg11.CHW.Pump1_Start/>
PREFIX ns4: <http://buildsys.org/ontologies/bldg11#bldg11.CHW.Pump2_Start/>
PREFIX owl: <http://www.w3.org/2002/07/owl#>
PREFIX qudt: <http://qudt.org/schema/qudt/>
PREFIX qudtqk: <http://qudt.org/vocab/quantitykind/>
PREFIX rdf: <http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#>
PREFIX rdfs: <http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#>
PREFIX rec: <https://w3id.org/rec#>
PREFIX ref: <https://brickschema.org/schema/Brick/ref#>
PREFIX s223: <http://data.ashrae.org/standard223#>
PREFIX sdo: <http://schema.org/>
PREFIX sh: <http://www.w3.org/ns/shacl#>
PREFIX skos: <http://www.w3.org/2004/02/skos/core#>
PREFIX sosa: <http://www.w3.org/ns/sosa/>
PREFIX tag: <https://brickschema.org/schema/BrickTag#>
PREFIX unit: <http://qudt.org/vocab/unit/>
PREFIX vcard: <http://www.w3.org/2006/vcard/ns#>
PREFIX xsd: <http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#>

# This query retrieves each Device along with its associated measurement and control points.
# It assumes that measurement instruments are modeled as brick:MeasurementPoint and control values as brick:ControlPoint,
# with points related to the device via the brick:hasPoint relationship. Adjust the classes or predicates as necessary to match your dataset.

SELECT ?device (GROUP_CONCAT(DISTINCT ?measurementPoint; separator=", ") AS ?measurementInstruments) (GROUP_CONCAT(DISTINCT ?controlPoint; separator=", ") AS ?controlValues)
WHERE {
  ?device a brick:Device .

  OPTIONAL {
    ?device brick:hasPoint ?measurementPoint .
    ?measurementPoint a brick:MeasurementPoint .
  }

  OPTIONAL {
    ?device brick:hasPoint ?controlPoint .
    ?controlPoint a brick:ControlPoint .
  }
}
GROUP BY ?device
ORDER BY ?device
